examples.title: Process Creation Service
examples.description: Demonstrates how OS creates a new process using fork() system call
examples.code:
FUNCTION CreateProcess(programName)
  BEGIN
    // Allocate Process Control Block (PCB)
    pcb = AllocatePCB()
    
    // Initialize PCB fields
    pcb.processID = GenerateUniqueID()
    pcb.processState = NEW
    pcb.programCounter = 0
    pcb.registers = InitializeRegisters()
    pcb.memoryMap = AllocateMemory(programSize)
    pcb.openFiles = InitializeFileTable()
    pcb.parentProcess = CurrentProcess()
    
    // Load program into memory
    LoadProgram(programName, pcb.memoryMap)
    
    // Add to process scheduling queue
    AddToReadyQueue(pcb)
    
    // Set process state to ready
    pcb.processState = READY
    
    RETURN pcb.processID
  END
examples.explanation: This pseudocode shows the Process Management service creating a new process. The OS allocates a Process Control Block (PCB), initializes all necessary fields, loads the program into memory, and adds the process to the ready queue for CPU scheduling.

examples.title: Memory Allocation Service
examples.description: Demonstrates dynamic memory allocation using malloc()
examples.explanation: This C code demonstrates the Memory Management service in action. The malloc() function requests memory from the OS, which tracks allocation through its memory management subsystem. The free() function releases memory back to the system for reuse.
examples.spaceComplexity: O(n) where n is requested size

examples.title: File Management Service
examples.description: Demonstrates file operations provided by OS file management service
examples.code:
import os

# File Management Service Operations

# 1. Create a new file
file = open("data.txt", "w")
file.write("Operating System Services Demo\n")
file.close()

# 2. Open and read file
file = open("data.txt", "r")
content = file.read()
print(content)
file.close()

# 3. Append to file
file = open("data.txt", "a")
file.write("Appended content\n")
file.close()

# 4. Get file information
stats = os.stat("data.txt")
print(f"File size: {stats.st_size} bytes")
print(f"Last modified: {stats.st_mtime}")

# 5. Delete file
os.remove("data.txt")
print("File deleted successfully")
examples.explanation: This Python code demonstrates File Management service. The OS provides file operations (create, open, read, write, delete) through system calls. It maintains file metadata (size, permissions, timestamps) and manages the file system structure.
examples.timeComplexity: O(1) for metadata, O(n) for read/write

examples.title: Inter-Process Communication - Message Passing
examples.description: Demonstrates communication service using message passing between processes
examples.explanation: This pseudocode demonstrates the Communication service using message passing. The OS kernel acts as an intermediary, copying messages between address spaces of sender and receiver processes. This provides process isolation and safe communication.
examples.spaceComplexity: O(m) where m is message size
